mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return frequency in declining-rate periods is not significantly above the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.3pp UNRATE trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the trigger fires with insufficient frequency (<5 occurrences) in the walk-forward sample to support inference.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal robust demand financed by cheap credit, a combination that sustains equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon; neither series alone captures this credit-financed demand channel.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-growth/low-FEDFUNDS condition shows no significant improvement in 60d forward return t-stat versus the RSAFS-only single-factor model, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity utilization pressures that precede input-cost inflation and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon; the same INDPRO rise in early-cycle recovery instead signals healthy growth and produces positive returns, making the effect regime-dependent.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3% while UNRATE below 5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During low-UNRATE, high-INDPRO-growth periods, forward 60d SPX returns are positive with t-stat > 2, contradicting the late-cycle margin-compression thesis, or the regime split shows no directional flip versus early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects sustained labor demand and consumer income generation, providing a short-horizon momentum tailwind that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign-hit rate does not exceed 52% out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above a threshold signals an inflation surprise that raises expectations of imminent monetary tightening, triggering a short-horizon risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following triggered CPI surprises, forward 5d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or fewer than 5 trigger events occur in the walk-forward sample, preventing reliable inference.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with a low or falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal easy-credit-driven demand expansion in a rate-sensitive sector, a leading indicator that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days; HOUST rises without accommodative rates do not carry the same signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change > 5% while FEDFUNDS below its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: The joint HOUST/FEDFUNDS trigger produces forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from the HOUST-only signal (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime combining elevated FEDFUNDS levels with rising UNRATE, tightening monetary policy compounds labor market weakness, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon; the same UNRATE rise under an accommodative FEDFUNDS regime does not carry the same downside signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 3% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with rising unemployment
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following joint trigger events are not significantly more negative than returns following UNRATE-only rises in low-FEDFUNDS regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.10), or trigger occurs fewer than 4 times in the walk-forward sample.
